review_text: Absolutely beautiful white sandy beaches with clear turquoise waters. Lovely camping spot and very clean area. They have toilets and bins. It can be windy. Spectacular sunrises and sunsets. They have a shipwrecked boat when it’s low tide. Little island has so many different species of birds, seals, dolphins etc. Could easy stayed a few days here.
Stayed on 11 n 12 site access to beach unreal 👌
Beautiful white sand, clear water with a good stretch of beach.  Camping option just above beach on levelled gravel surfaces.
Great spot for caravans, camper trailers etc. The beach is beautiful, water calm and crystal clear. Flies pretty bad in December. Bird life fantastic.
lovely spot but unfortunately to many bloody flies

WOW.

Such a beautiful spot.   Only did 1 night here but could easily stay here as week.   Need to book spot online with South Australia park website.   Mid week we were the only ones there.

Nice spot.
Just got to.watch the snakes in warmer weather

Wow, what a nice camp ground. Windy thought but great fir fishing and admiring the local birdlike.
Dog friendly, please keep on leash as lots of local wildlife.
Book through council website, $10 per night.

The local council has done a great job for camping down here. Segregated areas, toilets and fresh water. This place has always been so relaxing for a walk along the beach with squeaky sand. Yes... squeaky sand
Lol
Loved it!

Great spot for some off grid camping.  The best views sadly are spoilt by a council sign right in the middle of your views to the bird sanctuary and the water!!(site 12)
If the sign was lower or even attached to the fence would be some much better!!!
Top place $10 night

Beautiful sandy beach,smaller camping ground ,bookings now online,great kayaking around the small island,bird santuary,toilets 🚻 available otherwise you need to be fully self sufficient,2nd stay just as good 👍 enjoy🐚🐚🐚
We had a wonderful stay there. We were lucky enough to have a couple of picture perfect days. Bins and toilets. Will go back. Was lucky enough to see some dolphins.
Unreal spot. One of our favourites on the Eyre. Fishing, kayaks … plenty to do. Snakes too.
Excellent, just be mindful of the wind.

An amazing camp on an awesome beach. Spotless, smell free toilets and lots of rubbish bins. Only $10 per night. Great road in and plenty of room for larger caravans.Site 12 is the only site right on the water but 9, 10, 11 and 12 have views. Other sites are close to the beach but obstructed views. DO NOT BOOK SITE 3 - it is on the edge of the access road.

We have stayed here before as it is such a lovely spot, but this time we did not stay. A total waste of money. Do not choose site 3 whatever you do as this is on the road and very unsafe, if a car comes around the corner at night it is very dangerous. It is an accident waiting to happen. It is very uneven and not level at all and has holes at the side that is also dangerous.
Perfect camping spot.

Beautiful views just be careful Don't book site 3 as it is on the side of the main track in and is very uneven, other sites look good , didn't stay as site 3 is awful,  needs to be removed, would be very dangerous if you had small children

What a ripper,  $10 a site per night,  beautiful beaches with white, clean sand,  dump toilets and rubbish bins,  dolphins everywhere that will swim up to you in knee deep water,  birdlife is amazing,  have heard there is great fishing but didn't have any luck ourselves,
Just drove there for the views. Stunning
Brilliant location next to beach. Ground is very hard not good for tents
Came across this out of the main stream camping right on the beach front had fire a couple tinnies
Wonderful and quite. Little phone service
